name: Milk Chocolate and Orange Truffles
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a cookie sheet with foil.
2. Bring cream to simmer in a heavy medium saucepan.
3. Reduce heat to low and add half the chocolate, whisking until melted.
4. Whisk in butter and orange peel.
5. Freeze mixture until firm enough to mound on spoon, about 40 minutes.
6. Drop mixture by rounded tablespoons onto foil-lined sheet, spacing apart.
7. Freeze until almost firm but still pliable, about 30 minutes.
8. Roll each truffle in cocoa powder.
9. Roll between palms of hands into balls and place on the same sheet.
10. Freeze until firm, about 1 hour.
11. Melt remaining half of chocolate with shortening in a double boiler over simmering water, stirring until smooth.
12. Remove mixture from heat and grasp one truffle between thumb and index finger; roll truffle in melted chocolate, coating completely. Shake to remove excess chocolate.
13. Place truffle on the same foil-lined sheet. Repeat with remaining truffles.
14. Refrigerate until coating is firm, about 1 hour.
15. Dust truffles with cocoa powder and brush off excess. (Can be prepared up to 2 weeks ahead. Store in refrigerator in an airtight container.) Let stand at room temperature for 10 minutes before serving.
